name: add_fact
description: Add a new atomic fact to a knowledge graph entity. Use this when you have a new piece of information to record about a person, project, or concept. Each fact gets an auto-incremented ID (e.g., safa-001). Do not use this to update existing facts—use supersede_fact instead. Facts are stored in items.json and used to generate summaries.

entity.description: Entity path in format 'kind/slug', e.g., 'people/safa' or 'projects/clawdbot'. The entity must already exist under life/areas/.

category.description: Fact category. Valid values include 'status', 'project', 'preference', or similar categories already used by the entity.

fact.description: The fact content as a single atomic statement. Use a concise, factual sentence.

source.description: Source of the fact. Valid values: 'conversation', 'manual', 'inference'. Defaults to 'conversation' if not specified.

name: supersede_fact
description: Supersede an existing fact by marking it as superseded and adding a new fact. Use this when information has changed and you want to maintain history rather than deleting facts. The old fact's status changes to 'superseded' and links to the new fact via supersededBy. Do not use this for adding completely new facts—use add_fact instead.

old.description: ID of the fact to supersede, e.g., 'safa-002'. Must be an existing active fact ID for this entity.

category.description: Category for the new fact. Should match the category of the superseded fact or a valid alternative.

fact.description: The new fact content as a single atomic statement. Use a concise, factual sentence.

name: summarize_entity
description: Regenerate an entity summary markdown file (summary.md) from all active (non-superseded) facts. Use this to create or refresh a human-readable overview of an entity. The summary includes the entity name, update date, and bullet points of active facts sorted by timestamp. Do not use this to add or modify facts—use add_fact or supersede_fact instead.

entity.description: Entity path in format 'kind/slug', e.g., 'people/safa' or 'projects/clawdbot'. The entity must already exist under life/areas/ with at least one active fact.
